topic: LLM Agent Framework for Autonomous SPH-based Sloshing Simulation
motivation: SPH+LLM agent combination is a complete blue ocean — no existing research. SPH's most active industrial domain is sloshing (LNG tanks, fuel tanks, seismic). Current workflow requires deep domain expertise for case setup, creating high barrier for non-specialist engineers. This agent-based approach can solve real pain points of sloshing domain practitioners.
hypothesis: A domain-specialized LLM agent with SPH-specific tool interfaces can autonomously configure, execute, and analyze DualSPHysics sloshing simulations at expert-level accuracy, validated against SPHERIC benchmarks.
existing_approaches: ChatGPT/GPT-4 for general code generation (no CFD/SPH domain specialization)
AutoCFD/ML-augmented CFD (mesh optimization, surrogate models — mesh-based, not SPH)
LLM-based scientific workflow automation (ChemCrow for chemistry, Coscientist for chemistry)
Traditional GUI-based CFD setup (DesignSPHysics for DualSPHysics — no LLM)
Toolformer (Schick et al.) — general LLM tool use, no scientific simulation

available_data: SPHERIC Benchmark FTP data (Test 10: 100 repetitions, 3 fluids, pressure time series)
7 published paper PDFs with experimental data (Chen2018, Liu2024, English2021, ISOPE2012, NASA2023, Frosina2018, Zhao2024)
17 pre-run simulation result directories (8 PASS, 2 PARTIAL)
Additional benchmarks/simulations TBD after gap analysis
researcher_capabilities.compute_resources: NVIDIA RTX 4090 (24GB VRAM), CUDA 12.6, DualSPHysics v5.4 GPU solver in Docker, local Ollama (Qwen3 32B/8B)
existing_code: slosim-agent: Go+BubbleTea TUI with 14 DualSPHysics tools, Qwen3 32B/8B local LLM
pv-agent: ParaView MCP server for post-processing/rendering
Docker pipeline: GenCase → DualSPHysics GPU → PartVTK → pvpython → ffmpeg
accessible_benchmarks: SPHERIC Test 10 (water, oil, sunflower oil — FTP raw data)
Liu2024 pitch sloshing (3 fill x 5 freq x 3 amp)

constraints: Local LLM only (Qwen3 32B/8B via Ollama) — no cloud API dependency
Single GPU (RTX 4090) — simulation scale limited to ~500K particles
DualSPHysics v5.4 specific — SPH solver, not mesh-based CFD
research_questions: RQ1: How accurately can an LLM agent generate valid DualSPHysics XML configurations from natural language sloshing scenario descriptions?
RQ2: Can agent-driven simulations reproduce SPHERIC Test 10 benchmark results within acceptable error margins?
RQ3: What tool interface design patterns enable reliable LLM-SPH solver integration for particle-based simulation workflows?
RQ4: How does SPH domain-specialized prompting improve agent performance compared to generic LLM agents (ablation)?
known_gaps: Zero existing research on SPH + LLM agent combination
LLM tool-use for particle-based CFD solvers completely unexplored
Domain-specialized prompting for computational mechanics lacks systematic evaluation
No agent framework bridges NL input to validated SPH simulation output
key_references: English et al. 2021 — mDBC sloshing validation with DualSPHysics
Chen et al. 2018 — OpenFOAM sloshing parametric study
Liu et al. 2024 — Large tank pitch sloshing
Bran et al. 2024 — ChemCrow: LLM agent for chemistry
Boiko et al. 2023 — Coscientist: AI-driven scientific discovery
Schick et al. 2024 — Toolformer: LLMs learn to use tools
expected_contributions: First LLM agent system for SPH sloshing simulation automation
Tool interface design pattern for LLM-particle solver integration
Benchmark evaluation: agent vs expert on SPHERIC Test 10
Domain-specialized prompt engineering ablation for computational mechanics
Proof-of-concept for solving real sloshing practitioner pain points
novelty_claim: TBD after gap analysis (ZERO stage) — preliminary: First domain-specialized LLM agent for autonomous SPH sloshing simulation pipeline.
